 {"id":"git_safety_guard-du4","title":"Implement language detection from command context","description":"# Implement Language Detection from Command Context

## Objective

Implement the language detection logic that determines which parser/patterns to use for heredoc content.

## Background

Based on the design (`git_safety_guard-jfj`), implement the multi-source language detection:

1. Command prefix lookup
2. Shebang detection
3. Content heuristics (fallback)

## Implementation Details

### Language Enum

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub enum Language {
    Python,
    Bash,
    JavaScript,
    TypeScript,
    Ruby,
    Perl,
    Php,
    Unknown,
}

impl Language {
    /// Get the tree-sitter/ast-grep language identifier
    pub fn grammar_name(&self) -> Option<&'static str> {
        match self {
            Self::Python => Some("python"),
            Self::Bash => Some("bash"),
            Self::JavaScript => Some("javascript"),
            Self::TypeScript => Some("typescript"),
            Self::Ruby => Some("ruby"),
            Self::Perl => Some("perl"),
            Self::Php => Some("php"),
            Self::Unknown => None,
        }
    }
}
```

### Command Prefix Mapping

```rust
static COMMAND_LANGUAGE_MAP: LazyLock<HashMap<&'static str, Language>> = LazyLock::new(|| {
    let mut m = HashMap::new();
    // Python
    m.insert("python", Language::Python);
    m.insert("python3", Language::Python);
    m.insert("python2", Language::Python);
    // Bash/Shell
    m.insert("bash", Language::Bash);
    m.insert("sh", Language::Bash);
    m.insert("zsh", Language::Bash);
    m.insert("ksh", Language::Bash);
    // JavaScript/Node
    m.insert("node", Language::JavaScript);
    m.insert("nodejs", Language::JavaScript);
    // ... etc
    m
});

pub fn detect_from_command(cmd: &str) -> Option<Language> {
    // Extract first word (command name)
    let first_word = cmd.split_whitespace().next()?;
    // Handle paths: /usr/bin/python3 -> python3
    let basename = first_word.rsplit('/').next()?;
    COMMAND_LANGUAGE_MAP.get(basename).copied()
}
```

### Shebang Detection

```rust
pub fn detect_from_shebang(content: &str) -> Option<Language> {
    let first_line = content.lines().next()?;
    if !first_line.starts_with("#!") {
        return None;
    }
    // Parse: #!/usr/bin/env python3 -> python3
    // Parse: #!/bin/bash -> bash
    let interpreter = first_line
        .strip_prefix("#!")?
        .trim()
        .split_whitespace()
        .last()?
        .rsplit('/')
        .next()?;
    COMMAND_LANGUAGE_MAP.get(interpreter).copied()
}
```

### Content Heuristics (Fallback)

```rust
pub fn detect_from_content(content: &str) -> Option<Language> {
    // Very basic heuristics - only if nothing else works
    let lines: Vec<&str> = content.lines().take(10).collect();

    // Python indicators
    if lines.iter().any(|l| l.starts_with("import ") || l.starts_with("from ")) {
        return Some(Language::Python);
    }

    // JavaScript/Node indicators
    if lines.iter().any(|l| l.contains("require(") || l.contains("const ") || l.contains("let ")) {
        return Some(Language::JavaScript);
    }

    // Ruby indicators
    if lines.iter().any(|l| l.starts_with("def ") && content.contains("end")) {
        return Some(Language::Ruby);
    }

    None
}
```

### Combined Detection

```rust
pub fn detect_language(cmd: &str, heredoc: &HeredocContent) -> Language {
    // Priority 1: Command prefix
    if let Some(lang) = detect_from_command(cmd) {
        return lang;
    }

    // Priority 2: Shebang
    if let Some(lang) = detect_from_shebang(&heredoc.content) {
        return lang;
    }

    // Priority 3: Content heuristics
    if let Some(lang) = detect_from_content(&heredoc.content) {
        return lang;
    }

    Language::Unknown
}
```

## Testing

- Test command prefix for all supported commands
- Test shebang detection with various formats
- Test content heuristics
- Test fallback chain

## Acceptance Criteria

- Implements the priority order defined in `git_safety_guard-jfj` deterministically.
- Correctly identifies language for the majority of representative fixtures and returns `Unknown` for genuinely unknown cases.
- No panics on malformed input; bounded heuristics (only small prefix of content inspected).
- Provides enough diagnostics for `dcg explain` to show *why* a language was chosen (or why it was unknown).

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-dxs","title":"[CATEGORY] platform.github - GitHub Platform Pack","description":"# GitHub Platform Pack

## Overview
GitHub is the primary code hosting platform for most developers. Actions, webhooks, deploy keys, and repository settings can cause significant damage if misconfigured or deleted.

## Why This Pack
- GitHub Actions secrets contain sensitive credentials
- Deploy keys provide repository access
- Webhook deletion breaks CI/CD pipelines
- Repository deletion is catastrophic
- Branch protection removal exposes main branch

## Key Destructive Patterns

### gh CLI
- `gh repo delete` - Repository deletion
- `gh secret remove` - Removes Actions secrets
- `gh variable remove` - Removes Actions variables
- `gh repo deploy-key delete` - Removes deploy keys
- `gh api -X DELETE` - Raw API deletions
- `gh release delete` - Deletes releases
- `gh run cancel` - Cancels workflow runs

### API Patterns
- `DELETE /repos/{owner}/{repo}`
- `DELETE /repos/.../actions/secrets/`
- `DELETE /repos/.../hooks/`
- `DELETE /repos/.../keys/`

## Safe Patterns
- `gh repo view`, `gh repo list`
- `gh secret list`, `gh variable list`
- `gh api` with GET method
- `gh status`, `gh auth status`

## Dependencies
- Depends on: Tier 2 Epic (git_safety_guard-hhh)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] gh CLI patterns comprehensive
- [ ] API endpoint patterns detected
- [ ] Tests cover common destructive operations
- [ ] Safe operations not blocked","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:28:23.042704709-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T23:05:24.108230595-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T23:05:24.108230595-05:00","close_reason":"platform.github pack implemented and registered, all dependencies closed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-dxs","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-3w5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:34:29.797817078-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-dxs","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.494880713-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-dxs","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.54457833-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-dxs","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.590843759-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-dxs","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.636752267-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-dxs","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.680932382-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-dxs","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.726444594-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-e2eh","title":"Extend e2e_test.sh with heredoc test cases","description":"# E2E Test Script Extension for Heredocs

## Objective

Extend the existing `scripts/e2e_test.sh` with comprehensive heredoc test cases.

## Test Categories to Add

### 1. Heredoc Detection Tests

```bash
# Should BLOCK - Python heredoc with os.system
test_blocked 'python3 << EOF
import os
os.system("rm -rf /tmp/test")
EOF' "Python heredoc with os.system"

# Should BLOCK - Bash heredoc with rm
test_blocked 'bash << EOF
rm -rf /important
EOF' "Bash heredoc with rm -rf"

# Should BLOCK - Node inline script
test_blocked 'node -e "require(\x27child_process\x27).execSync(\x27rm -rf /\x27)"' "Node inline with execSync"
```

### 2. Heredoc Variant Tests

```bash
# Test all heredoc syntaxes
test_blocked 'cat <<EOF | python3
import os; os.system("rm -rf /")
EOF' "Piped heredoc"

test_blocked 'python3 <<-EOF
    import os
    os.system("rm -rf /")
EOF' "Indented heredoc (<<-)"

test_blocked 'python3 <<\x27EOF\x27
import os
os.system("rm -rf /")
EOF' "Quoted delimiter heredoc"
```

### 3. Language Coverage Tests

```bash
# Python
test_blocked 'python3 -c "import shutil; shutil.rmtree(\x27/tmp\x27)"'

# Ruby  
test_blocked 'ruby -e "FileUtils.rm_rf(\x27/tmp\x27)"'

# Perl
test_blocked 'perl -e "system(\x27rm -rf /\x27)"'

# JavaScript
test_blocked 'node -e "require(\x27fs\x27).rmdirSync(\x27/\x27, {recursive:true})"'
```

### 4. False Positive Tests

```bash
# Should ALLOW - Documentation with dangerous strings
test_allowed 'bd create --description="This pattern blocks rm -rf"' "Doc with rm -rf"
test_allowed 'git commit -m "Fix git reset --hard detection"' "Commit msg"
test_allowed 'grep "rm -rf" src/main.rs' "Grep for pattern"
test_allowed 'echo "example: git push --force"' "Echo example"
```

### 5. Bypass Attempt Tests

```bash
# Variable indirection
test_blocked 'bash -c "CMD=rm; $CMD -rf /"' "Variable indirection"

# Base64 encoding
test_blocked 'bash -c "echo cm0gLXJmIC8K | base64 -d | bash"' "Base64 bypass"

# String concatenation  
test_blocked 'python3 -c "exec(\x27r\x27+\x27m\x27+\x27 -rf /\x27)"' "String concat"
```

### 6. Edge Cases

```bash
# Empty heredoc
test_allowed 'cat << EOF
EOF' "Empty heredoc"

# Safe heredoc content
test_allowed 'python3 << EOF
print("hello world")
EOF' "Safe Python heredoc"

# Nested quotes
test_blocked 'bash -c "bash -c \x27rm -rf /\x27"' "Nested shells"
```

## Logging Enhancement

Add verbose mode:
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

VERBOSE=${VERBOSE:-0}
LOG_FILE="e2e_test_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"

log() {
    echo "[$(date '+%H:%M:%S')] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

test_blocked() {
    local cmd="$1"
    local desc="$2"
    log "TEST: $desc"
    log "  CMD: ${cmd:0:80}..."

    if echo "{\"tool_name\":\"Bash\",\"tool_input\":{\"command\":\"$cmd\"}}" | ./target/release/dcg 2>/dev/null; then
        log "  FAIL: Expected block, got allow"
        return 1
    else
        log "  PASS: Correctly blocked"
        return 0
    fi
}
```

## Output Summary

At end of test run:
```
=====================================
E2E Test Summary
=====================================
Total tests: 150
Passed: 148
Failed: 2
Skipped: 0

Failed tests:
  - Python heredoc with subprocess (line 234)
  - Node inline with dynamic require (line 456)

Log file: e2e_test_20260107_193045.log
=====================================
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- `scripts/e2e_test.sh` contains a dedicated heredoc section with:
  - true-positive blocks across at least 3 languages
  - false-positive regression cases (doc/test strings)
  - a small set of bypass-attempt cases
- Verbose logging is available and includes per-test:
  - test name
  - normalized command (truncated)
  - decision (allow/deny) and rule attribution when denied
  - failure diagnostics sufficient for CI triage
- The heredoc E2E suite runs deterministically and completes in a reasonable time budget (define target after baseline).
","notes":"Adding multi-language heredoc/inline E2E coverage + logging improvements now that e7m is integrated.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonFalcon","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:00:00-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T06:52:18.249005975-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T06:52:18.249005975-05:00","close_reason":"Expanded scripts/e2e_test.sh heredoc/inline suite to cover multi-language blocks (node/python/ruby/perl/bash), added safe heredoc cases + safe-string trigger regression, and improved verbose logs to surface rule attribution from denial reason.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-e2eh","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T19:28:32.841249285-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-e2eh","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:08:55.268336107-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-e2eh","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-yza","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:50:08.884862029-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-e4fl","title":"AI Agent Ergonomics & MCP Integration","description":"# Epic: AI Agent Ergonomics & MCP Integration

## Priority Score: 750/1000 (High Priority)

## Background and Rationale

DCG is a security tool designed for AI coding agents. The research found that modern AI agents (Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI) expect:
1. Structured JSON output with predictable schemas
2. Clear, actionable error messages
3. Consistent exit codes
4. MCP-compatible tool interfaces
5. Machine-readable documentation

This epic ensures DCG is a first-class citizen in the AI coding agent ecosystem.

## Research Findings (2025-2026)

### Claude Code Hook Protocol
- Exit 0: Normal operation (check JSON for decision)
- Exit 2: Blocking error (stderr shown to Claude)
- JSON output only processed on exit 0
- permissionDecision: allow | deny | ask

### Emerging Standards
- AGENTS.md: Machine-readable context for AI agents (20k+ repos)
- Agent Client Protocol (ACP): JSON-RPC over stdio
- MCP November 2025 spec: Structured tool outputs with JSON Schema

### Key Patterns
- --format json flag on all commands
- Structured error objects with codes
- Remediation suggestions in JSON
- Idempotent operations

## Technical Approach

1. Enhance HookSpecificOutput struct with new fields (ruleId, severity, remediation)
2. Add JSON Schema validation for output
3. Create AGENTS.md file for DCG
4. Add --format json to all subcommands
5. Standardize error codes and messages
6. Consider MCP server mode for direct integration

## Key Files

- src/hook.rs - Enhance HookSpecificOutput
- src/cli.rs - Add --format json to subcommands
- AGENTS.md - New: Machine-readable agent guide
- src/mcp.rs - New: Optional MCP server mode
- docs/json-schema.json - New: JSON Schema for outputs

## Success Criteria

- All commands support --format json
- JSON output includes ruleId, severity, remediation
- AGENTS.md provides agent-friendly documentation
- Error codes are consistent and documented
- MCP server mode works (optional)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T14:19:30.487385802-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T14:19:30.487385802-05:00"}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-e4fl.1","title":"[Agent] Enhance HookSpecificOutput with ruleId, severity, remediation","description":"Enhance HookSpecificOutput with additional fields for better agent integration.

## New Fields

Add to src/hook.rs HookSpecificOutput:
- ruleId: String (e.g., "core.git:reset-hard")
- packId: String (e.g., "core.git")
- severity: Severity enum (low/medium/high/critical)
- confidence: f64 (0.0-1.0)
- remediation: Option<Remediation> struct
  - safeAlternative: Option<String>
  - explanation: String
  - allowOnceCommand: String

## Backward Compatibility

New fields should use skip_serializing_if to maintain backward compat.

## Acceptance Criteria

- HookSpecificOutput includes all new fields
- JSON output validates against updated schema
- Existing hook consumers not broken","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T14:19:54.023131526-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T17:40:19.956581145-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T17:40:19.956581145-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented HookSpecificOutput enhancements with ruleId, packId, severity, confidence, remediation fields. - Include code blocks agents can parse
